one_liner: AI-powered route optimisation for mid-market logistics companies
overview: RoutePilot AI builds an AI-powered route optimisation engine for mid-market logistics companies (50-500 vehicles). Their software integrates with existing TMS platforms and claims to reduce route planning time by 80% and improve delivery efficiency by 18-30%. The product targets a segment underserved by enterprise solutions (too expensive) and manual planning (too slow).

market.segment: Mid-market fleet operators (50-500 vehicles), ~12,000 companies in DACH alone
market.trends: Regulatory pressure on emissions reporting, driver shortage forcing efficiency gains, legacy TMS vendors slow to adopt AI

team.background: 12 years in logistics tech. Previously VP Product at a Series B fleet management company (acquired 2022). MBA from WHU.
PhD in Operations Research (TU Berlin). 3 patents in combinatorial route optimisation. Previously senior engineer at HERE Technologies.
8 years in enterprise SaaS sales. Previously at Samsara (logistics IoT). Built DACH sales from 0 to EUR 2M ARR.

lead_thesis.title: Why Kreuzberg Seed Partners invested
lead_thesis.content: We believe the mid-market logistics segment is fundamentally underserved by current software. Enterprise TMS vendors (Oracle, SAP) price out this segment, while most operators still plan routes manually or with basic tools. RoutePilot's approach - integrating as a layer on top of existing TMS rather than replacing it - reduces adoption friction dramatically. The founding team combines deep domain expertise with strong technical capability. Our key conviction: the 18-30% efficiency gain claim is backed by 6 months of customer data across 3 fleet sizes. Our key uncertainty: whether the sales motion can scale beyond founder-led selling. The VP Sales hire is promising but unproven at this stage.
open_questions_from_lead: Can the sales motion scale beyond founder-led deals?
Will mid-market operators pay EUR 2,500/month for optimisation software?
How defensible is the AI model against incumbents adding similar features?

dimensions.explanation: You have 20 years in logistics operations - you can evaluate their product claims from direct experience
Your 200+ warehouse partnerships include the exact mid-market operators RoutePilot targets
You could introduce RoutePilot to your DACH logistics network as both a channel and a validation partner
Your operational experience lets you validate whether their efficiency claims hold in real fleet environments
You have no logistics-AI exposure in your current portfolio - this fills a strategic gap

strategic_fit.what_you_contribute: Beyond capital, you bring domain credibility, a potential pilot customer base, and distribution access across DACH mid-market logistics operators.

diligence_status.note: Strong NPS, confirmed efficiency gains of 15-22%
Unit economics confirmed by independent review
No direct mid-market competitor in DACH
Integration with 2 of 5 target TMS platforms confirmed; remaining 3 in progress

risks.detail: Lead's customer calls confirmed 15-22% gains, below the marketed 30%. Needs broader validation.
All 18 customers acquired through founder-led sales. VP Sales joined 3 months ago. No outbound pipeline data yet.
risks.what_resolves_it: 3-6 months of outbound sales data post-VP Sales hire

risks.detail: Product value depends on integrating with incumbent TMS. Only 2 of 5 target integrations are complete.
risks.what_resolves_it: Completion of top 3 TMS integrations before round close

risks.detail: Oracle and SAP could add AI routing features. But their sales motion does not target mid-market, and enterprise product cycles are 18-24 months.
risks.what_resolves_it: Speed to market and customer lock-in through data network effects
